assertions.title: Service errors are logged, not silently swallowed

steps.action: Run targeted pytest logging/silent-swallow checks in tests/test_services.py
steps.expected: Tests confirm exceptions are logged and silent swallow patterns are absent

steps.action: Trigger a real service failure via invalid iMessage DB content
steps.expected: Service logs a structured error instead of silently swallowing
steps.observed: loguru ERROR line emitted: imsg_contacts failed ... sqlite3.DatabaseError: file is not a database

steps.action: Run rg for except.*pass in services.py

evidence.keyResults: 11 targeted services tests passed; explicit ERROR log captured on forced failure

steps.expected: Sequential calls reuse a cached SQLite connection
steps.observed: Test passed with connect_calls == 1 across two notes_list() calls

steps.expected: Connection creation centralized in connection manager
steps.observed: Single connect site found in SQLiteConnectionManager.get_connection

evidence.keyResults: Pooling behavior validated by unit test and connect pattern check

steps.expected: Batch API is used with fewer HTTP calls than contacts
steps.observed: Test passed with batch_request_count == 1, standalone_metadata_calls == 0, http_call_count == 2

steps.expected: Batch API call site exists in Gmail listing path
steps.observed: Matches at lines guarding and invoking service.new_batch_http_request(...)

evidence.keyResults: Batch metadata fetch path and behavior confirmed

steps.expected: Token refresh writes are lock-protected and concurrency-safe
steps.observed: Both token locking tests passed, including concurrent write JSON integrity check

steps.expected: File lock operations wrap token writes
steps.observed: fcntl.flock lock/unlock and _write_text_with_lock usage at token write sites found

evidence.keyResults: Token write locking implemented and exercised in tests

steps.expected: Special chars are escaped and sanitizer is used in AppleScript call paths
steps.observed: All three sanitizer/call-site tests passed for quotes, backslashes, braces, newline/tab, and Unicode

steps.action: Run rg subprocess.*osascript and _escape_applescript/safe_* in services.py
steps.expected: AppleScript callsites correlate with sanitized interpolations
steps.observed: osascript callsites and sanitizer-backed safe_* interpolations present in iMessage/notes/reminders paths

evidence.keyResults: Dedicated sanitizer exists and is verified by unit tests

assertions.title: InboxClient.close() is called on TUI exit

steps.expected: Client close is called during quit/unmount lifecycle
steps.observed: Both tests passed and assert close() called once

steps.action: Run rg on_unmount|client.close|_client.close across inbox.py and inbox_client.py
steps.expected: TUI and client close call path exists
steps.observed: inbox.py calls self.client.close() and defines on_unmount; inbox_client.py close() calls self._client.close()

evidence.keyResults: Shutdown cleanup path validated by tests and code-path grep

steps.action: Run poll interval tests in tests/test_inbox_app.py
steps.expected: Env override/default/invalid handling and timer usage are validated
steps.observed: All selected poll interval tests passed, including _start_polling uses app.POLL_INTERVAL

steps.expected: Configurable poll interval source and use are visible
steps.observed: inbox.py reads INBOX_POLL_INTERVAL with default and uses POLL_INTERVAL in set_interval

steps.expected: Locked DB is handled gracefully with warning and empty result
steps.observed: Test passed asserting notes_list() returns [] and warning log text includes database is locked

steps.action: Run rg database is locked in services.py
steps.observed: Lock detection string checks found in _is_sqlite_locked_error

evidence.keyResults: Locked SQLite handling behavior confirmed by unit test

frictions.description: Contract evidence string rg 'except.*:\s*$' is over-broad and matches non-silent, correctly-logged except clauses.
frictions.resolution: Used targeted pytest assertions plus explicit rg 'except.*pass' and real failure-trigger log evidence to validate non-silent error handling.

summary: Tested 8 assigned architecture-hardening CLI assertions; all 8 passed using targeted pytest runs plus rg/python code-path and runtime-log evidence.
